nodes.label: AI as concise planning tool
nodes.grounding_span: just for the AI to make a little lesson schedule and spit it back to me

nodes.label: AI writing style alignment with user preference
nodes.label_negative: AI writing style mismatch (overly simplistic or colorful)
nodes.grounding_span: I don't like the way AI writes...the word choices and sentence structure feels 'too perfect'

nodes.label: AI as jumping-off point for lesson planning
nodes.label_negative: AI as direct content generator to be copied
nodes.grounding_span: I never copy and paste from AI and instead try to use it as a jumping-off point

nodes.label: AI compatibility with pedagogical depth and standards
nodes.label_negative: AI oversimplification or neglect of key concepts
nodes.grounding_span: would be simplified down too much to allow us to understand the underlying scientific principles

nodes.label: AI creativity and novelty
nodes.label_negative: AI reliance on existing data limiting novelty
nodes.grounding_span: AI 'creativity' doesn't really help me...it's hard to generate an idea that's really novel

nodes.label: AI alignment with educational authenticity values
nodes.label_negative: AI use conflicting with academic honesty expectations
nodes.grounding_span: schools are expected to have a level of academic honesty and authenticity above a corporate setting

nodes.label: Efficiency and time-saving in lesson planning
nodes.grounding_span: if I have a 50 minute class period...just for the AI to make a little lesson schedule and spit it back to me

nodes.label: Pedagogical integrity and conceptual depth
nodes.grounding_span: I like to build my lessons in a way that reinforces previous lessons

nodes.label: Personal creativity and authentic expression
nodes.grounding_span: I write and paint as hobbies...I want to tailor them to my class and my students

nodes.label: Professional authenticity and ethical standards
nodes.grounding_span: schools are expected to have a level of academic honesty and authenticity

nodes.label: Frustration with AI verbosity and format mismatch
nodes.grounding_span: Having to parse a 5-paragraph response for just the information I want is less useful

nodes.label: Skepticism toward AI writing style
nodes.grounding_span: I don't like the way AI writes...it conflates 'simple terms' with 'young audience'

nodes.label: Preference for AI as starting point, not final product
nodes.grounding_span: I never copy and paste from AI and instead try to use it as a jumping-off point

nodes.label: Dissatisfaction with AI pedagogical depth
nodes.grounding_span: no matter what AI gave me, it felt clunky

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in creative and personalized tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to do any kind of writing or creative work myself

nodes.label: Concern about AI undermining skill value in society
nodes.grounding_span: I fear that these skills will become less desired by employers and by society

nodes.label: Conditional openness to future AI use if improved
nodes.grounding_span: If an AI chatbot comes out that gives me amazing responses...I will probably use it a lot more

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through back-and-forth prompting
nodes.grounding_span: I often have to go back in forth after that to get a response that's usable for me

nodes.label: Preference for minimal, direct output without elaboration
nodes.grounding_span: I don't need a bulleted list, I don't need explanations, I don't need a 'helpful tips' section
